data.description:
# Behavioral-comparison completion verifier + ticket-scope-drift detection for rebar's gates

Captured as an undesigned idea from session log **c905-a38c-0419-448e** (alias
`apt-otter-urn`), authored by Joe Oakhart. This is the third of three proposals in
that log; the other two became epics `elfish-chair-lug` (88ab, feature-branch
accumulation) and `woozy-pea-kin` (66ac, completion-aware container plan-review).
This proposal is **designed at the investigation level but not yet decomposed or
plan-reviewed** â€” it is parked here for later design/refinement.

## Context (why this investigation was run)

The goal was to make rebar's LLM review gates more *accurate* (fewer false
positives, and to detect scope creep), rather than to change the multi-story
landing workflow. Two gates were examined: the **completion verifier**
(closes a work ticket) and the **Gerrit code-review bot** (votes on a diff).

## Findings (code)

- **Completion verifier** (`src/rebar/llm/completion.py` +
  `reviewers/completion_verifier.md`) judges a ticket against the pinned HEAD tree
  with read-only tools; it does **NOT** use commit trailers / `file_impact` /
  `verify_commands` for ticketâ†”code association.
- **Gerrit review-bot** reviews the diff in isolation: the `{{ticket_context}}`
  prompt slots are filled with the **diff** (`batch_runner.py:58`,
  `code-review.yaml:63-64`); the voter never extracts the `rebar-ticket` trailer or
  passes `target_ticket`, even though `produce_code_review_verdict` accepts one and
  the attested-tickets store is already materialized.
- **`verify-commit-ticket`** checks only that the id resolves â€” no content / status
  / claimant check.
- **Net gap:** no gate checks that a diff *semantically serves* its named ticket;
  no scope-creep detection anywhere.

## Research

- **Jin & Chen, ASE 2025 (arXiv:2508.12358)** â€” a **Behavioral Comparison Prompt**
  (blind spec-summary + blind impl-summary, *then* compare) lifts correct-code
  recognition from **52% â†’ 85â€“90%**. Rich single-pass judging (rebar's current
  verifier shape) is the documented false-positive failure mode.
- **BitsAI-CR** â€” an independent verify stage lifts precision **25% â†’ 75%**;
  conclusion-first output.
- **Qodo / CodeRabbit** â€” diffâ†”linked-ticket compliance is the production
  scope-check pattern; no published detector research â€” `file_impact` is a unique
  deterministic prior rebar already has.
- **Google Tricorder** â€” advisory-first, ~10% "not-useful" demotion budget,
  blocking only for near-deterministic / execution-verified checks.

## Proposal (4 parts)

1. **Restructure `completion-verification.yaml` into 3 steps:**
   - `spec_summary` (ticket only),
   - `impl_summary` (code only; worklist = `file_impact` + trailer-linked paths,
     ticket prose withheld),
   - `compare` (1-TURN, conclusion-first).
   Keep `reconcile_verdict` + signing unchanged.

2. **Wire `target_ticket` into the Gerrit voter** (`extract_ticket_refs` already
   exists) + a new **advisory ticket-alignment LLM overlay** + a **prompt-less DET
   criterion** for "files-outside-`file_impact`" in
   `code_review/criteria_routing.json`.

3. **Asymmetric posture:** under-delivery can **FAIL** (as today); over-delivery is
   **advisory-only in v1**, with a coach message naming
   `discovered_from` / `file_impact` / AC-amend as the cure. Scope amendments that
   only **ADD** `file_impact` should qualify for the `drift_refresh` lightweight
   re-sign path (`attest.py`) so they don't stale the plan-review attestation.
   Enforce a per-criterion noise budget (~10% override rate â†’ demote) before any
   blocking promotion.

4. **Advisory ticket-status warning in `verify-commit-ticket`.** Untrailered
   commits are **not** chased (the completion verifier reads the final tree, so
   correctness doesn't depend on trailer coverage).

## Rollout

plumbing + DET â†’ behavioral-comparison verifier (eval before/after on
`eval_specs` fixtures) â†’ ticket-alignment overlay advisory + telemetry â†’ earned
per-criterion blocking.

## Acceptance Criteria (to be refined during design â€” this is an idea, not yet dispatchable)

- [ ] `completion-verification.yaml` restructured into blind `spec_summary` /
      `impl_summary` / conclusion-first `compare` steps; `reconcile_verdict` +
      signing behavior unchanged.
- [ ] Before/after eval on `eval_specs` fixtures demonstrates the FP-reduction the
      behavioral-comparison research predicts.
- [ ] `target_ticket` extracted + passed through the Gerrit voter; advisory
      ticket-alignment overlay + prompt-less DET "files-outside-`file_impact`"
      criterion added under `code_review/criteria_routing.json`.
- [ ] Asymmetric posture implemented (under-delivery FAIL / over-delivery advisory
      v1); `file_impact`-only scope additions route to the `drift_refresh`
      lightweight re-sign.
- [ ] Per-criterion ~10% not-useful demotion budget + telemetry in place before any
      criterion is promoted to blocking.
- [ ] Advisory ticket-status warning added to `verify-commit-ticket`.

## Source

Verbatim from session log c905-a38c-0419-448e, comment by Joe Oakhart
(2026-07-07). Not claimed / not implemented / not plan-reviewed â€” a parked idea.
data.title: Behavioral-comparison completion verifier + ticket-scope-drift detection for rebar's gates
